translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was perfect and upright, and one that feared God, and eschewed evil.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there were born unto him seven sons and three daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His substance also was seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred yoke of oxen, and five hundred she asses, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the men of the east.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And his sons went and feasted in their houses, every one his day; and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it was so, when the days of their feasting were gone about, that Job sent and sanctified them, and rose up early in the morning, and offered burnt offerings according to the number of them all: for Job said, It may be that my sons have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts. Thus did Job continually.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan came also among them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Satan, Whence comest thou? Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Satan answered the LORD, and said, Doth Job fear God for nought?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast not thou made an hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side? thou hast blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is increased in the land.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But put forth thine hand now, and touch all that he hath, and he will curse thee to thy face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Satan, Behold, all that he hath is in thy power; only upon himself put not forth thine hand. So Satan went forth from the presence of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there came a messenger unto Job, and said, The oxen were plowing, and the asses feeding beside them:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Sabeans fell upon them, and took them away; yea, they have slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The fire of God is fallen from heaven, and hath burned up the sheep, and the servants, and consumed them; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, The Chaldeans made out three bands, and fell upon the camels, and have carried them away, yea, and slain the servants with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, there came also another, and said, Thy sons and thy daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother’s house:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And, behold, there came a great wind from the wilderness, and smote the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men, and they are dead; and I only am escaped alone to tell thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job arose, and rent his mantle, and shaved his head, and fell down upon the ground, and worshipped,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And said, Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, and naked shall I return thither: the LORD gave, and the LORD hath taken away; blessed be the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In all this Job sinned not, nor charged God foolishly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan came also among them to present himself before the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered the LORD, and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? and still he holdeth fast his integrity, although thou movedst me against him, to destroy him without cause.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Satan answered the LORD, and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But put forth thine hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse thee to thy face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Satan, Behold, he is in thine hand; but save his life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So went Satan forth from the presence of the LORD, and smote Job with sore boils from the sole of his foot unto his crown.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he took him a potsherd to scrape himself withal; and he sat down among the ashes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then said his wife unto him, Dost thou still retain thine integrity? curse God, and die.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But he said unto her, Thou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh. What? shall we receive good at the hand of God, and shall we not receive evil? In all this did not Job sin with his lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him, they came every one from his own place; Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite: for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice, and wept; and they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So they sat down with him upon the ground seven days and seven nights, and none spake a word unto him: for they saw that his grief was very great.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: After this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Job spake, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said, There is a man child conceived.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let that day be darkness; let not God regard it from above, neither let the light shine upon it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let darkness and the shadow of death stain it; let a cloud dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As for that night, let darkness seize upon it; let it not be joined unto the days of the year, let it not come into the number of the months.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo, let that night be solitary, let no joyful voice come therein.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark; let it look for light, but have none; neither let it see the dawning of the day:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because it shut not up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why died I not from the womb? why did I not give up the ghost when I came out of the belly?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For now should I have lain still and been quiet, I should have slept: then had I been at rest,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With kings and counsellors of the earth, which built desolate places for themselves;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The small and great are there; and the servant is free from his master.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery, and life unto the bitter in soul;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which long for death, but it cometh not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why is light given to a man whose way is hid, and whom God hath hedged in?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For my sighing cometh before I eat, and my roarings are poured out like the waters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I was not in safety, neither had I rest, neither was I quiet; yet trouble came.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not this thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion’s whelps are scattered abroad.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image was before mine eyes, there was silence, and I heard a voice, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the saints wilt thou turn?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither is there any to deliver them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They meet with darkness in the day time, and grope in the noonday as in the night.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, happy is the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle shall be in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great, and thine offspring as the grass of the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age, like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo this, we have searched it, so it is; hear it, and know thou it for thy good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Oh that my grief were throughly weighed, and my calamity laid in the balances together!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea: therefore my words are swallowed up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, the poison whereof drinketh up my spirit: the terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? or loweth the ox over his fodder?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Can that which is unsavoury be eaten without salt? or is there any taste in the white of an egg?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The things that my soul refused to touch are as my sorrowful meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Oh that I might have my request; and that God would grant me the thing that I long for!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even that it would please God to destroy me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then should I yet have comfort; yea, I would harden myself in sorrow: let him not spare; for I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should prolong my life?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is my strength the strength of stones? or is my flesh of brass?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not my help in me? and is wisdom driven quite from me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To him that is afflicted pity should be shewed from his friend; but he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook, and as the stream of brooks they pass away;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which are blackish by reason of the ice, and wherein the snow is hid:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What time they wax warm, they vanish: when it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The troops of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba waited for them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They were confounded because they had hoped; they came thither, and were ashamed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For now ye are nothing; ye see my casting down, and are afraid.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Did I say, Bring unto me? or, Give a reward for me of your substance?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or, Deliver me from the enemy’s hand? or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue: and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How forcible are right words! but what doth your arguing reprove?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Do ye imagine to reprove words, and the speeches of one that is desperate, which are as wind?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and ye dig a pit for your friend.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now therefore be content, look upon me; for it is evident unto you if I lie.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Return, I pray you, let it not be iniquity; yea, return again, my righteousness is in it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is there iniquity in my tongue? cannot my taste discern perverse things?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is there not an appointed time to man upon earth? are not his days also like the days of an hireling?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As a servant earnestly desireth the shadow, and as an hireling looketh for the reward of his work:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I lie down, I say, When shall I arise, and the night be gone? and I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and become loathsome.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle, and are spent without hope.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O remember that my life is wind: mine eye shall no more see good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away: so he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him any more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Am I a sea, or a whale, that thou settest a watch over me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I say, My bed shall comfort me, my couch shall ease my complaint;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then thou scarest me with dreams, and terrifiest me through visions:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So that my soul chooseth strangling, and death rather than my life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I loathe it; I would not live alway: let me alone; for my days are vanity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What is man, that thou shouldest magnify him? and that thou shouldest set thine heart upon him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And that thou shouldest visit him every morning, and try him every moment?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How long wilt thou not depart from me, nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have sinned; what shall I do unto thee, O thou preserver of men? why hast thou set me as a mark against thee, so that I am a burden to myself?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And why dost thou not pardon my transgression, and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I sleep in the dust; and thou shalt seek me in the morning, but I shall not be.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How long wilt thou speak these things? and how long shall the words of thy mouth be like a strong wind?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth God pervert judgment? or doth the Almighty pervert justice?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thy children have sinned against him, and he have cast them away for their transgression;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou wouldest seek unto God betimes, and make thy supplication to the Almighty;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou wert pure and upright; surely now he would awake for thee, and make the habitation of thy righteousness prosperous.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though thy beginning was small, yet thy latter end should greatly increase.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For enquire, I pray thee, of the former age, and prepare thyself to the search of their fathers:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: (For we are but of yesterday, and know nothing, because our days upon earth are a shadow:)

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall not they teach thee, and tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Can the rush grow up without mire? can the flag grow without water?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whilst it is yet in his greenness, and not cut down, it withereth before any other herb.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So are the paths of all that forget God; and the hypocrite’s hope shall perish:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whose hope shall be cut off, and whose trust shall be a spider’s web.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall lean upon his house, but it shall not stand: he shall hold it fast, but it shall not endure.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If he destroy him from his place, then it shall deny him, saying, I have not seen thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the earth shall others grow.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, God will not cast away a perfect man, neither will he help the evil doers:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Till he fill thy mouth with laughing, and thy lips with rejoicing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame; and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I know it is so of a truth: but how should man be just with God?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and hath prospered?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which commandeth the sun, and it riseth not; and sealeth up the stars.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the waves of the sea.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which maketh Arcturus, Orion, and Pleiades, and the chambers of the south.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which doeth great things past finding out; yea, and wonders without number.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo, he goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If God will not withdraw his anger, the proud helpers do stoop under him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How much less shall I answer him, and choose out my words to reason with him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whom, though I were righteous, yet would I not answer, but I would make supplication to my judge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I had called, and he had answered me; yet would I not believe that he had hearkened unto my voice.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he breaketh me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He will not suffer me to take my breath, but filleth me with bitterness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I speak of strength, lo, he is strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time to plead?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I justify myself, mine own mouth shall condemn me: if I say, I am perfect, it shall also prove me perverse.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though I were perfect, yet would I not know my soul: I would despise my life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This is one thing, therefore I said it, He destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If the scourge slay suddenly, he will laugh at the trial of the innocent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The earth is given into the hand of the wicked: he covereth the faces of the judges thereof; if not, where, and who is he?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now my days are swifter than a post: they flee away, they see no good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are passed away as the swift ships: as the eagle that hasteth to the prey.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my heaviness, and comfort myself:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am afraid of all my sorrows, I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I be wicked, why then labour I in vain?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I wash myself with snow water, and make my hands never so clean;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet shalt thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes shall abhor me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him, and we should come together in judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Neither is there any daysman betwixt us, that might lay his hand upon us both.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his fear terrify me:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then would I speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My soul is weary of my life; I will leave my complaint upon myself; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will say unto God, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou contendest with me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thine hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man’s days,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That thou enquirest after mine iniquity, and searchest after my sin?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and hast fenced me with bones and sinews.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou hast granted me life and favour, and thy visitation hath preserved my spirit.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And these things hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this is with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I be wicked, woe unto me; and if I be righteous, yet will I not lift up my head. I am full of confusion; therefore see thou mine affliction;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For it increaseth. Thou huntest me as a fierce lion: and again thou shewest thyself marvellous upon me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war are against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Are not my days few? cease then, and let me alone, that I may take comfort a little,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Before I go whence I shall not return, even to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If he cut off, and shut up, or gather together, then who can hinder him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass’s colt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that pass away:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday: thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: No doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright man is laughed to scorn.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them again.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo, mine eye hath seen all this, mine ear hath heard and understood it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, do ye so mock him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He will surely reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Your remembrances are like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what will.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He also shall be my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold now, I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is he that will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he, as a rotten thing, consumeth, as a garment that is moth eaten.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Man that is born of a woman is of few days, and full of trouble.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down: he fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months are with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though the root thereof wax old in the earth, and the stock thereof die in the ground;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet through the scent of water it will bud, and bring forth boughs like a plant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But man dieth, and wasteth away: yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where is he?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So man lieth down, and riseth not: till the heavens be no more, they shall not awake, nor be raised out of their sleep.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave, that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If a man die, shall he live again? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt call, and I will answer thee: thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My transgression is sealed up in a bag, and thou sewest up mine iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And surely the mountain falling cometh to nought, and the rock is removed out of his place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The waters wear the stones: thou washest away the things which grow out of the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not of them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Art thou the first man that was born? or wast thou made before the hills?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With us are both the grayheaded and very aged men, much elder than thy father.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What is man, that he should be clean? and he which is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How much more abominable and filthy is man, which drinketh iniquity like water?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will shew thee, hear me; and that which I have seen I will declare;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which wise men have told from their fathers, and have not hid it:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Unto whom alone the earth was given, and no stranger passed among them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The wicked man travaileth with pain all his days, and the number of years is hidden to the oppressor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A dreadful sound is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He wandereth abroad for bread, saying, Where is it? he knoweth that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He runneth upon him, even on his neck, upon the thick bosses of his bucklers:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because he covereth his face with his fatness, and maketh collops of fat on his flanks.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he dwelleth in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heaps.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the congregation of hypocrites shall be desolate, and fire shall consume the tabernacles of bribery.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They conceive mischief, and bring forth vanity, and their belly prepareth deceit.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I also could speak as ye do: if your soul were in my soul’s stead, I could heap up words against you, and shake mine head at you.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips should asswage your grief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though I speak, my grief is not asswaged: and though I forbear, what am I eased?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou hast filled me with wrinkles, which is a witness against me: and my leanness rising up in me beareth witness to my face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He breaketh me with breach upon breach, he runneth upon me like a giant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Not for any injustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O earth, cover not thou my blood, and let my cry have no place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my record is on high.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man pleadeth for his neighbour!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that speaketh flattery to his friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find one wise man among you.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, even the thoughts of my heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How long will it be ere ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed vile in your sight?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He teareth himself in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of his place?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The gin shall take him by the heel, and the robber shall prevail against him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It shall devour the strength of his skin: even the firstborn of death shall devour his strength.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His confidence shall be rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because it is none of his: brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name in the street.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall neither have son nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They that come after him shall be astonied at his day, as they that went before were affrighted.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked, and this is the place of him that knoweth not God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How long will ye vex my soul, and break me in pieces with words?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These ten times have ye reproached me: ye are not ashamed that ye make yourselves strange to me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And be it indeed that I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me, and plead against me my reproach:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Know now that God hath overthrown me, and hath compassed me with his net.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, I cry out of wrong, but I am not heard: I cry aloud, but there is no judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath fenced up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath destroyed me on every side, and I am gone: and mine hope hath he removed like a tree.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His troops come together, and raise up their way against me, and encamp round about my tabernacle.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are verily estranged from me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My kinsfolk have failed, and my familiar friends have forgotten me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They that dwell in mine house, and my maids, count me for a stranger: I am an alien in their sight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I called my servant, and he gave me no answer; I intreated him with my mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My breath is strange to my wife, though I intreated for the children’s sake of mine own body.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All my inward friends abhorred me: and they whom I loved are turned against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, O ye my friends; for the hand of God hath touched me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why do ye persecute me as God, and are not satisfied with my flesh?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Oh that my words were now written! oh that they were printed in a book!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That they were graven with an iron pen and lead in the rock for ever!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I know that my redeemer liveth, and that he shall stand at the latter day upon the earth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And though after my skin worms destroy this body, yet in my flesh shall I see God:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another; though my reins be consumed within me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But ye should say, Why persecute we him, seeing the root of the matter is found in me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Be ye afraid of the sword: for wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword, that ye may know there is a judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore do my thoughts cause me to answer, and for this I make haste.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have heard the check of my reproach, and the spirit of my understanding causeth me to answer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Knowest thou not this of old, since man was placed upon earth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That the triumphing of the wicked is short, and the joy of the hypocrite but for a moment?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though his excellency mount up to the heavens, and his head reach unto the clouds;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet he shall perish for ever like his own dung: they which have seen him shall say, Where is he?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall fly away as a dream, and shall not be found: yea, he shall be chased away as a vision of the night.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eye also which saw him shall see him no more; neither shall his place any more behold him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His children shall seek to please the poor, and his hands shall restore their goods.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His bones are full of the sin of his youth, which shall lie down with him in the dust.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth, though he hide it under his tongue;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though he spare it, and forsake it not; but keep it still within his mouth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet his meat in his bowels is turned, it is the gall of asps within him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath swallowed down riches, and he shall vomit them up again: God shall cast them out of his belly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall suck the poison of asps: the viper’s tongue shall slay him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall not see the rivers, the floods, the brooks of honey and butter.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That which he laboured for shall he restore, and shall not swallow it down: according to his substance shall the restitution be, and he shall not rejoice therein.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because he hath oppressed and hath forsaken the poor; because he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely he shall not feel quietness in his belly, he shall not save of that which he desired.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There shall none of his meat be left; therefore shall no man look for his goods.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When he is about to fill his belly, God shall cast the fury of his wrath upon him, and shall rain it upon him while he is eating.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall flee from the iron weapon, and the bow of steel shall strike him through.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is drawn, and cometh out of the body; yea, the glittering sword cometh out of his gall: terrors are upon him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The increase of his house shall depart, and his goods shall flow away in the day of his wrath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, and the heritage appointed unto him by God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Suffer me that I may speak; and after that I have spoken, mock on.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As for me, is my complaint to man? and if it were so, why should not my spirit be troubled?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Mark me, and be astonished, and lay your hand upon your mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even when I remember I am afraid, and trembling taketh hold on my flesh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore do the wicked live, become old, yea, are mighty in power?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Their seed is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They take the timbrel and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment go down to the grave.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore they say unto God, Depart from us; for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what profit should we have, if we pray unto him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How oft is the candle of the wicked put out! and how oft cometh their destruction upon them! God distributeth sorrows in his anger.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For what pleasure hath he in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off in the midst?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His breasts are full of milk, and his bones are moistened with marrow.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, I know your thoughts, and the devices which ye wrongfully imagine against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For ye say, Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Have ye not asked them that go by the way? and do ye not know their tokens,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction? they shall be brought forth to the day of wrath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him, and every man shall draw after him, as there are innumerable before him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous? or is it gain to him, that thou makest thy ways perfect?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou hast taken a pledge from thy brother for nought, and stripped the naked of their clothing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But as for the mighty man, he had the earth; and the honourable man dwelt in it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou hast sent widows away empty, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or darkness, that thou canst not see; and abundance of waters cover thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not God in the height of heaven? and behold the height of the stars, how high they are!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou sayest, How doth God know? can he judge through the dark cloud?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh in the circuit of heaven.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which were cut down out of time, whose foundation was overflown with a flood:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which said unto God, Depart from us: and what can the Almighty do for them?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet he filled their houses with good things: but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The righteous see it, and are glad: and the innocent laugh them to scorn.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whereas our substance is not cut down, but the remnant of them the fire consumeth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Acquaint now thyself with him, and be at peace: thereby good shall come unto thee.

translations.name: King James Version
