translations.text: Receive, I pray thee, the law from his mouth, and lay up his words in thine heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up, thou shalt put away iniquity far from thy tabernacles.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then shalt thou lay up gold as dust, and the gold of Ophir as the stones of the brooks.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, the Almighty shall be thy defence, and thou shalt have plenty of silver.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For then shalt thou have thy delight in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face unto God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him, and he shall hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee: and the light shall shine upon thy ways.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When men are cast down, then thou shalt say, There is lifting up; and he shall save the humble person.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But he is in one mind, and who can turn him? and what his soul desireth, even that he doeth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why, seeing times are not hidden from the Almighty, do they that know him not see his days?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, as wild asses in the desert, go they forth to their work; rising betimes for a prey: the wilderness yieldeth food for them and for their children.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They reap every one his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that they have no covering in the cold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They cause him to go naked without clothing, and they take away the sheaf from the hungry;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which make oil within their walls, and tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy, and in the night is as a thief.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth his face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the dark they dig through houses, which they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the morning is to them even as the shadow of death: if one know them, they are in the terrors of the shadow of death.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is swift as the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Drought and heat consume the snow waters: so doth the grave those which have sinned.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He evil entreateth the barren that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no man is sure of life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are exalted for a little while, but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way as all other, and cut off as the tops of the ears of corn.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And if it be not so now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Dominion and fear are with him, he maketh peace in his high places.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold even to the moon, and it shineth not; yea, the stars are not pure in his sight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How much less man, that is a worm? and the son of man, which is a worm?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Job answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As for the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture’s eye hath not seen:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The lion’s whelps have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed by it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The depth saith, It is not in me: and the sea saith, It is not with me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom is above rubies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, neither shall it be valued with pure gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Destruction and death say, We have heard the fame thereof with our ears.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then did he see it, and declare it; he prepared it, yea, and searched it out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the LORD, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days when God preserved me;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When his candle shined upon my head, and when by his light I walked through darkness;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret of God was upon my tabernacle;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the Almighty was yet with me, when my children were about me;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I washed my steps with butter, and the rock poured me out rivers of oil;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I went out to the gate through the city, when I prepared my seat in the street!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The young men saw me, and hid themselves: and the aged arose, and stood up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The princes refrained talking, and laid their hand on their mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The nobles held their peace, and their tongue cleaved to the roof of their mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the ear heard me, then it blessed me; and when the eye saw me, it gave witness to me:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me: and I caused the widow’s heart to sing for joy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me: my judgment was as a robe and a diadem.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I was a father to the poor: and the cause which I knew not I searched out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I brake the jaws of the wicked, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then I said, I shall die in my nest, and I shall multiply my days as the sand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My root was spread out by the waters, and the dew lay all night upon my branch.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My glory was fresh in me, and my bow was renewed in my hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Unto me men gave ear, and waited, and kept silence at my counsel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: After my words they spake not again; and my speech dropped upon them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they waited for me as for the rain; and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I laughed on them, they believed it not; and the light of my countenance they cast not down.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I chose out their way, and sat chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth the mourners.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots for their meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They were driven forth from among men, (they cried after them as after a thief;)

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To dwell in the cliffs of the valleys, in caves of the earth, and in the rocks.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They were children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because he hath loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loose the bridle before me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, they have no helper.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it, and dissolvest my substance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I looked for good, then evil came unto me: and when I waited for light, there came darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I went mourning without the sun: I stood up, and I cried in the congregation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owls.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My skin is black upon me, and my bones are burned with heat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My harp also is turned to mourning, and my organ into the voice of them that weep.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For what portion of God is there from above? and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth not he see my ways, and count all my steps?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I have walked with vanity, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let me be weighed in an even balance that God may know mine integrity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then let me sow, and let another eat; yea, let my offspring be rooted out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If mine heart have been deceived by a woman, or if I have laid wait at my neighbour’s door;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then let my wife grind unto another, and let others bow down upon her.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For this is an heinous crime; yea, it is an iniquity to be punished by the judges.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction, and would root out all mine increase.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I did despise the cause of my manservant or of my maidservant, when they contended with me;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What then shall I do when God riseth up? and when he visiteth, what shall I answer him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not one fashion us in the womb?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or have eaten my morsel myself alone, and the fatherless hath not eaten thereof;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: (For from my youth he was brought up with me, as with a father, and I have guided her from my mother’s womb;)

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or any poor without covering;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If his loins have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, when I saw my help in the gate:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then let mine arm fall from my shoulder blade, and mine arm be broken from the bone.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For destruction from God was a terror to me, and by reason of his highness I could not endure.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I have made gold my hope, or have said to the fine gold, Thou art my confidence;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because mine hand had gotten much;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I beheld the sun when it shined, or the moon walking in brightness;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This also were an iniquity to be punished by the judge: for I should have denied the God that is above.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by wishing a curse to his soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If the men of my tabernacle said not, Oh that we had of his flesh! we cannot be satisfied.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The stranger did not lodge in the street: but I opened my doors to the traveller.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I covered my transgressions as Adam, by hiding mine iniquity in my bosom:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Did I fear a great multitude, or did the contempt of families terrify me, that I kept silence, and went not out of the door?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire is, that the Almighty would answer me, and that mine adversary had written a book.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely I would take it upon my shoulder, and bind it as a crown to me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If my land cry against me, or that the furrows likewise thereof complain;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, or have caused the owners thereof to lose their life:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let thistles grow instead of wheat, and cockle instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young, and ye are very old; wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, I attended unto you, and, behold, there was none of you that convinced Job, or that answered his words:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still, and answered no more;)

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I said, I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, my belly is as wine which hath no vent; it is ready to burst like new bottles.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let me not, I pray you, accept any man’s person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou canst answer me, set thy words in order before me, stand up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, I am according to thy wish in God’s stead: I also am formed out of the clay.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I am clean without transgression, I am innocent; neither is there iniquity in me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, in this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong pain:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his uprightness:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His flesh shall be fresher than a child’s: he shall return to the days of his youth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou hast anything to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Furthermore Elihu answered and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the ear trieth words, as the mouth tasteth meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let us choose to us judgment: let us know among ourselves what is good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For Job hath said, I am righteous: and God hath taken away my judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Should I lie against my right? my wound is incurable without transgression.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What man is like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore hearken unto me ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, surely God will not do wickedly, neither will the Almighty pervert judgment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath given him a charge over the earth? or who hath disposed the whole world?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All flesh shall perish together, and man shall turn again unto dust.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall even he that hateth right govern? and wilt thou condemn him that is most just?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is it fit to say to a king, Thou art wicked? and to princes, Ye are ungodly?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How much less to him that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich more than the poor? for they all are the work of his hands.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In a moment shall they die, and the people shall be troubled at midnight, and pass away: and the mighty shall be taken away without hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There is no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall break in pieces mighty men without number, and set others in their stead.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore he knoweth their works, and he overturneth them in the night, so that they are destroyed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because they turned back from him, and would not consider any of his ways:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That the hypocrite reign not, lest the people be ensnared.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely it is meet to be said unto God, I have borne chastisement, I will not offend any more:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That which I see not teach thou me: if I have done iniquity, I will do no more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Should it be according to thy mind? he will recompense it, whether thou refuse, or whether thou choose; and not I: therefore speak what thou knowest.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words were without wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My desire is that Job may be tried unto the end because of his answers for wicked men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth his hands among us, and multiplieth his words against God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Elihu spake moreover, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more than God’s?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For thou saidst, What advantage will it be unto thee? and, What profit shall I have, if I be cleansed from my sin?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Look unto the heavens, and see; and behold the clouds which are higher than thou.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? or if thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou be righteous, what givest thou him? or what receiveth he of thine hand?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; and thy righteousness may profit the son of man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By reason of the multitude of oppressions they make the oppressed to cry: they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But none saith, Where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowls of heaven?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There they cry, but none giveth answer, because of the pride of evil men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Although thou sayest thou shalt not see him, yet judgment is before him; therefore trust thou in him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But now, because it is not so, he hath visited in his anger; yet he knoweth it not in great extremity:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Elihu also proceeded, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that I have yet to speak on God’s behalf.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Maker.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For truly my words shall not be false: he that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, God is mighty, and despiseth not any: he is mighty in strength and wisdom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He preserveth not the life of the wicked: but giveth right to the poor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: but with kings are they on the throne; yea, he doth establish them for ever, and they are exalted.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And if they be bound in fetters, and be holden in cords of affliction;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions that they have exceeded.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If they obey and serve him, they shall spend their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But if they obey not, they shall perish by the sword, and they shall die without knowledge.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the hypocrites in heart heap up wrath: they cry not when he bindeth them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They die in youth, and their life is among the unclean.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even so would he have removed thee out of the strait into a broad place, where there is no straitness; and that which should be set on thy table should be full of fatness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But thou hast fulfilled the judgment of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold on thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because there is wrath, beware lest he take thee away with his stroke: then a great ransom cannot deliver thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will he esteem thy riches? no, not gold, nor all the forces of strength.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Desire not the night, when people are cut off in their place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take heed, regard not iniquity: for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, God exalteth by his power: who teacheth like him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath enjoined him his way? or who can say, Thou hast wrought iniquity?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Remember that thou magnify his work, which men behold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every man may see it; man may behold it afar off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, God is great, and we know him not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he maketh small the drops of water: they pour down rain according to the vapour thereof:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which the clouds do drop and distil upon man abundantly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also can any understand the spreadings of the clouds, or the noise of his tabernacle?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For by them judgeth he the people; he giveth meat in abundance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With clouds he covereth the light; and commandeth it not to shine by the cloud that cometh betwixt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The noise thereof sheweth concerning it, the cattle also concerning the vapour.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: At this also my heart trembleth, and is moved out of his place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear attentively the noise of his voice, and the sound that goeth out of his mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency; and he will not stay them when his voice is heard.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: God thundereth marvellously with his voice; great things doeth he, which we cannot comprehend.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For he saith to the snow, Be thou on the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then the beasts go into dens, and remain in their places.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Out of the south cometh the whirlwind: and cold out of the north.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By the breath of God frost is given: and the breadth of the waters is straitened.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also by watering he wearieth the thick cloud: he scattereth his bright cloud:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it is turned round about by his counsels: that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the world in the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He causeth it to come, whether for correction, or for his land, or for mercy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hearken unto this, O Job: stand still, and consider the wondrous works of God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Dost thou know when God disposed them, and caused the light of his cloud to shine?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him which is perfect in knowledge?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How thy garments are warm, when he quieteth the earth by the south wind?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou with him spread out the sky, which is strong, and as a molten looking glass?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Teach us what we shall say unto him; for we cannot order our speech by reason of darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall it be told him that I speak? if a man speak, surely he shall be swallowed up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now men see not the bright light which is in the clouds: but the wind passeth, and cleanseth them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Fair weather cometh out of the north: with God is terrible majesty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Touching the Almighty, we cannot find him out: he is excellent in power, and in judgment, and in plenty of justice: he will not afflict.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Gird up now thy loins like a man; for I will demand of thee, and answer thou me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? declare, if thou hast understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath laid the measures thereof, if thou knowest? or who hath stretched the line upon it?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whereupon are the foundations thereof fastened? or who laid the corner stone thereof;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Or who shut up the sea with doors, when it brake forth, as if it had issued out of the womb?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I made the cloud the garment thereof, and thick darkness a swaddlingband for it,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And brake up for it my decreed place, and set bars and doors,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And said, Hitherto shalt thou come, but no further: and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days; and caused the dayspring to know his place;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is turned as clay to the seal; and they stand as a garment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the high arm shall be broken.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Have the gates of death been opened unto thee? or hast thou seen the doors of the shadow of death?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou perceived the breadth of the earth? declare if thou knowest it all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Where is the way where light dwelleth? and as for darkness, where is the place thereof,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof, and that thou shouldest know the paths to the house thereof?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Knowest thou it, because thou wast then born? or because the number of thy days is great?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou entered into the treasures of the snow? or hast thou seen the treasures of the hail,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which I have reserved against the time of trouble, against the day of battle and war?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By what way is the light parted, which scattereth the east wind upon the earth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To cause it to rain on the earth, where no man is; on the wilderness, wherein there is no man;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: To satisfy the desolate and waste ground; and to cause the bud of the tender herb to spring forth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hath the rain a father? or who hath begotten the drops of dew?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Out of whose womb came the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who hath gendered it?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The waters are hid as with a stone, and the face of the deep is frozen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou bind the sweet influences of Pleiades, or loose the bands of Orion?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou bring forth Mazzaroth in his season? or canst thou guide Arcturus with his sons?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Knowest thou the ordinances of heaven? canst thou set the dominion thereof in the earth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou send lightnings, that they may go, and say unto thee, Here we are?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who can number the clouds in wisdom? or who can stay the bottles of heaven,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When the dust groweth into hardness, and the clods cleave fast together?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou hunt the prey for the lion? or fill the appetite of the young lions,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When they couch in their dens, and abide in the covert to lie in wait?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who provideth for the raven his food? when his young ones cry unto God, they wander for lack of meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? or canst thou mark when the hinds do calve?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou number the months that they fulfil? or knowest thou the time when they bring forth?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They bow themselves, they bring forth their young ones, they cast out their sorrows.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Their young ones are in good liking, they grow up with corn; they go forth, and return not unto them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath sent out the wild ass free? or who hath loosed the bands of the wild ass?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whose house I have made the wilderness, and the barren land his dwellings.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He scorneth the multitude of the city, neither regardeth he the crying of the driver.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The range of the mountains is his pasture, and he searcheth after every green thing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will the unicorn be willing to serve thee, or abide by thy crib?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou bind the unicorn with his band in the furrow? or will he harrow the valleys after thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou trust him, because his strength is great? or wilt thou leave thy labour to him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou believe him, that he will bring home thy seed, and gather it into thy barn?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Gavest thou the goodly wings unto the peacocks? or wings and feathers unto the ostrich?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Which leaveth her eggs in the earth, and warmeth them in dust,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And forgetteth that the foot may crush them, or that the wild beast may break them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She is hardened against her young ones, as though they were not hers: her labour is in vain without fear;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because God hath deprived her of wisdom, neither hath he imparted to her understanding.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What time she lifteth up herself on high, she scorneth the horse and his rider.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou make him afraid as a grasshopper? the glory of his nostrils is terrible.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He paweth in the valley, and rejoiceth in his strength: he goeth on to meet the armed men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He mocketh at fear, and is not affrighted; neither turneth he back from the sword.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The quiver rattleth against him, the glittering spear and the shield.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage: neither believeth he that it is the sound of the trumpet.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He saith among the trumpets, Ha, ha; and he smelleth the battle afar off, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth the hawk fly by thy wisdom, and stretch her wings toward the south?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Doth the eagle mount up at thy command, and make her nest on high?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: She dwelleth and abideth on the rock, upon the crag of the rock, and the strong place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: From thence she seeketh the prey, and her eyes behold afar off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Her young ones also suck up blood: and where the slain are, there is she.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Moreover the LORD answered Job, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, I am vile; what shall I answer thee? I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Once have I spoken; but I will not answer: yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then answered the LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou also disannul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hast thou an arm like God? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Deck thyself now with majesty and excellency; and array thyself with glory and beauty.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Cast abroad the rage of thy wrath: and behold every one that is proud, and abase him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Look on every one that is proud, and bring him low; and tread down the wicked in their place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hide them in the dust together; and bind their faces in secret.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then will I also confess unto thee that thine own right hand can save thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold now behemoth, which I made with thee; he eateth grass as an ox.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lo now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the navel of his belly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He moveth his tail like a cedar: the sinews of his stones are wrapped together.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His bones are as strong pieces of brass; his bones are like bars of iron.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is the chief of the ways of God: he that made him can make his sword to approach unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Surely the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He lieth under the shady trees, in the covert of the reed, and fens.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The shady trees cover him with their shadow; the willows of the brook compass him about.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, he drinketh up a river, and hasteth not: he trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He taketh it with his eyes: his nose pierceth through snares.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord which thou lettest down?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou put an hook into his nose? or bore his jaw through with a thorn?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will he make many supplications unto thee? will he speak soft words unto thee?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him for a servant for ever?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Shall the companions make a banquet of him? shall they part him among the merchants?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons? or his head with fish spears?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not one be cast down even at the sight of him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: None is so fierce that dare stir him up: who then is able to stand before me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who hath prevented me, that I should repay him? whatsoever is under the whole heaven is mine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will not conceal his parts, nor his power, nor his comely proportion.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who can discover the face of his garment? or who can come to him with his double bridle?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who can open the doors of his face? his teeth are terrible round about.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His scales are his pride, shut up together as with a close seal.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: One is so near to another, that no air can come between them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are joined one to another, they stick together, that they cannot be sundered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By his neesings a light doth shine, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Out of his mouth go burning lamps, and sparks of fire leap out.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a seething pot or caldron.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In his neck remaineth strength, and sorrow is turned into joy before him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His heart is as firm as a stone; yea, as hard as a piece of the nether millstone.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When he raiseth up himself, the mighty are afraid: by reason of breakings they purify themselves.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sword of him that layeth at him cannot hold: the spear, the dart, nor the habergeon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He esteemeth iron as straw, and brass as rotten wood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The arrow cannot make him flee: slingstones are turned with him into stubble.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Darts are counted as stubble: he laugheth at the shaking of a spear.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He maketh a path to shine after him; one would think the deep to be hoary.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I know that thou canst do every thing, and that no thought can be withholden from thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is he that hideth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore I abhor myself, and repent in dust and ashes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it was so, that after the LORD had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you: for him will I accept: lest I deal with you after your folly, in that ye have not spoken of me the thing which is right, like my servant Job.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD commanded them: the LORD also accepted Job.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He had also seven sons and three daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he called the name of the first, Jemima; and the name of the second, Kezia; and the name of the third, Kerenhappuch.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: After this lived Job an hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons’ sons, even four generations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So Job died, being old and full of days.
